panels.description:
Health status of the monitored object

-  **0**: The cluster is 100% available. -  **1**: All primary shards are allocated, but some replicas are missing. No data is missing, so search results will still be complete. However, high availability is compromised to some degree. If more shards disappear, you might lose data. Think of this status as a warning that should prompt investigation. -  **2**: Data is missing and the cluster cannot work properly. -  **3**: Failed to obtain the cluster status.
panels.title: Cluster Health Status

panels.description:
Circuit breaker status for vector indexes.

-  0: Not enabled. -  1: Enabled. Check whether the cluster's off-heap memory usage exceeds the threshold.
panels.title: Vector Index Circuit Breaker Status

panels.description: Total number of requests sent to all nodes in a CSS cluster.
panels.title: Total Requests

panels.description:
Number of requests successfully handled by all nodes in a CSS cluster.

Status code: 200.
panels.title: Successful requests

panels.description:
Number of failed requests for all nodes in a CSS cluster.

Status code: non-200.
panels.title: Failed requests

panels.description:
Number of throttled requests for all nodes in a CSS cluster.

Status code: 429.
panels.title: Throttled requests

panels.description: The total size of cold data stored in a CSS cluster, which is the total size of the data of frozen indexes stored in an OBS bucket. This metric is measured hourly. Users cannot check this OBS bucket on the OBS console.
panels.title: The size of cold data stored

panels.description: Number of index creation failures in a CSS cluster.
panels.title: Number of index creation failures

panels.description: Number of shards where the number of documents exceeds the threshold in a CSS cluster.
panels.title: Number of shards where the number of documents exceeds the threshold

panels.description:
Number of write failures caused by abnormal index aliases in a CSS cluster.

Only Elasticsearch 7.6.2 and 7.10.2 clusters support this metric.
panels.title: Number of Write Failures Due to Alias Exceptions

panels.description:
Number of write failures caused by too many index documents in a CSS cluster.

Only Elasticsearch 7.6.2 and 7.10.2 clusters support this metric.
panels.title: Number of Write Failures Due to Excessive Documents

panels.description:
Number of write failures caused by too many index fields in a CSS cluster.

Only Elasticsearch 7.6.2 and 7.10.2 clusters support this metric.
panels.title: Number of Write Failures Due to Excessive Fields

panels.description:
Number of write failures caused by too many index shards in a CSS cluster.

Only Elasticsearch 7.6.2 and 7.10.2 clusters support this metric.
panels.title: Number of Write Failures Due to Excessive Shards

panels.description:
Number of write failures caused by the setting of write prohibition in index configuration in a CSS cluster.

Only Elasticsearch 7.6.2 and 7.10.2 clusters support this metric.
panels.title: Number of Write Failures Due to Index Write Prohibition

panels.description:
Number of write failures caused by incorrect index field types in a CSS cluster.

Only Elasticsearch 7.6.2 and 7.10.2 clusters support this metric.
panels.title: Number of Write Failures Due to Incorrect Field Types

panels.description:
Number of primary/secondary synchronization jobs for a CSS cluster.

Only Elasticsearch 7.6.2 and 7.10.2 clusters support this metric.
panels.title: Number of Primary/Secondary Synchronization Jobs

panels.description:
Number of failed primary/secondary synchronization jobs for a CSS cluster.

Only Elasticsearch 7.6.2 and 7.10.2 clusters support this metric.

panels.description:
Total size of data to be synchronized via primary/secondary synchronization jobs for all primary shards in a CSS cluster.

panels.description:

panels.description:

panels.description:

panels.description:

panels.title: Number of Documents to Be Synchronized via Primary/Secondary Synchronization Jobs
